62_1_1.NKJV: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon, and our hands have handled, concerning the Word of life—
62_1_1.KJV: That which<S>3739</S> was<S>2258</S> from<S>575</S> the beginning<S>746</S>, which<S>3739</S> we have heard<S>191</S>, which<S>3739</S> we have seen<S>3708</S> with our<S>2257</S> eyes<S>3788</S>, which<S>3739</S> we have looked upon<S>2300</S>, and<S>2532</S> our<S>2257</S> hands<S>5495</S> have handled<S>5584</S>, of<S>4012</S> the Word<S>3056</S> of life<S>2222</S>;
62_1_1.ESV: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we looked upon and have touched with our hands, concerning the word of life —
62_1_1.NASB: What was from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have looked at and touched with our hands, concerning the Word of Life--
62_1_1.NLT: We proclaim to you the one who existed from the beginning, whom we have heard and seen. We saw him with our own eyes and touched him with our own hands. He is the Word of life.
62_1_1.WEB: That which was from the beginning, that which we have heard, that which we have seen with our eyes, that which we saw, and our hands touched, concerning the Word of life
62_1_1.CSB17: What was from the beginning, <sup>ⓐ</sup> what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have observed and have touched with our hands, concerning the word <sup>ⓑ</sup> of life —
62_1_1.AMP: [I am writing about] what existed from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have looked at and touched with our hands, concerning the Word of Life [the One who existed even before the beginning of the world, Christ]—
62_1_1.MSG: From the very first day, we were there, taking it all in—we heard it with our own ears, saw it with our own eyes, verified it with our own hands.
62_1_1.NIV:
The Word of Life
That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked at and our hands have touched—this we proclaim concerning the Word of life.
62_1_1.NRSVCE: We declare to you what was from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have looked at and touched with our hands, concerning the word of life —

62_1_2.NKJV: the life was manifested, and we have seen, and bear witness, and declare to you that eternal life which was with the Father and was manifested to us—
62_1_2.KJV: (For<S>2532</S> the life<S>2222</S> was manifested<S>5319</S>, and<S>2532</S> we have seen<S>3708</S> it, and<S>2532</S> bear witness<S>3140</S>, and<S>2532</S> shew<S>518</S> unto you<S>5213</S> that eternal<S>166</S> life<S>2222</S>, which<S>3748</S> was<S>2258</S> with<S>4314</S> the Father<S>3962</S>, and<S>2532</S> was manifested<S>5319</S> unto us<S>2254</S>;)
62_1_2.ESV: the life was made manifest, and we have seen it, and testify to it and proclaim to you the eternal life, which was with the Father and was made manifest to us —
62_1_2.NASB: and the life was manifested, and we have seen and testify and proclaim to you the eternal life, which was with the Father and was manifested to us--
62_1_2.NLT: This one who is life itself was revealed to us, and we have seen him. And now we testify and proclaim to you that he is the one who is eternal life. He was with the Father, and then he was revealed to us.
62_1_2.WEB: (and the life was revealed, and we have seen, and testify, and declare to you the life, the eternal life, which was with the Father, and was revealed to us);
62_1_2.CSB17: that life was revealed, <sup>ⓒ</sup> and we have seen it and we testify and declare to you the eternal life that was with the Father and was revealed to us <sup>ⓓ</sup>  —
62_1_2.AMP: and the Life [an aspect of His being] was manifested, and we have seen [it as eyewitnesses] and testify and declare to you [the Life], the eternal Life who was [already existing] with the Father and was [actually] made visible to us [His followers]—
62_1_2.MSG: The Word of Life appeared right before our eyes; we saw it happen! And now we're telling you in most sober prose that what we witnessed was, incredibly, this: The infinite Life of God himself took shape before us.
62_1_2.NIV: The life appeared; we have seen it and testify to it, and we proclaim to you the eternal life, which was with the Father and has appeared to us.
62_1_2.NRSVCE: this life was revealed, and we have seen it and testify to it, and declare to you the eternal life that was with the Father and was revealed to us —

62_1_3.NKJV: that which we have seen and heard we declare to you, that you also may have fellowship with us; and truly our fellowship is with the Father and with His Son Jesus Christ.
62_1_3.KJV: That which<S>3739</S> we have seen<S>3708</S> and<S>2532</S> heard<S>191</S> declare we<S>518</S> unto you<S>5213</S>, that<S>2443</S> ye<S>5210</S> also<S>2532</S> may have<S>2192</S> fellowship<S>2842</S> with<S>3326</S> us<S>2257</S>: and<S>2532</S> truly our<S>2251</S> fellowship<S>2842</S> <S>1161</S> is with<S>3326</S> the Father<S>3962</S>, and<S>2532</S> with<S>3326</S> his<S>846</S> Son<S>5207</S> Jesus<S>2424</S> Christ<S>5547</S>.
62_1_3.ESV: that which we have seen and heard we proclaim also to you, so that you too may have fellowship with us; and indeed our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.
62_1_3.NASB: what we have seen and heard we proclaim to you also, so that you too may have fellowship with us; and indeed our fellowship is with the Father, and with His Son Jesus Christ.
62_1_3.NLT: We proclaim to you what we ourselves have actually seen and heard so that you may have fellowship with us. And our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
62_1_3.WEB: that which we have seen and heard we declare to you, that you also may have fellowship with us. Yes, and our fellowship is with the Father, and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
62_1_3.CSB17: what we have seen and heard we also declare to you, so that you may also have fellowship <sup>ⓔ</sup> with us; and indeed our fellowship is with the Father <sup>ⓕ</sup> and with his Son Jesus Christ.
62_1_3.AMP: what we have seen and heard we also proclaim to you, so that you too may have fellowship [as partners] with us. And indeed our fellowship [which is a distinguishing mark of born-again believers] is with the Father, and with His Son Jesus Christ.
62_1_3.MSG: We saw it, we heard it, and now we're telling you so you can experience it along with us, this experience of communion with the Father and his Son, Jesus Christ.
62_1_3.NIV: We proclaim to you what we have seen and heard, so that you also may have fellowship with us. And our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
62_1_3.NRSVCE: we declare to you what we have seen and heard so that you also may have fellowship with us; and truly our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.

62_1_4.NKJV: And these things we write to you that your joy may be full.
62_1_4.KJV: And<S>2532</S> these things<S>5023</S> write we<S>1125</S> unto you<S>5213</S>, that<S>2443</S> your<S>5216</S> joy<S>5479</S> may be<S>5600</S> full<S>4137</S>.
62_1_4.ESV: And we are writing these things so that our  joy may be complete.
62_1_4.NASB: These things we write, so that our joy may be made complete.
62_1_4.NLT: We are writing these things so that you may fully share our joy.
62_1_4.WEB: And we write these things to you, that our joy may be fulfilled.
62_1_4.CSB17: We are writing these things <sup>[1]</sup> so that our <sup>[2]</sup> joy <sup>ⓖ</sup> may be complete.
62_1_4.AMP: We are writing these things to you so that our joy [in seeing you included] may be made complete [by having you share in the joy of salvation].
62_1_4.MSG: Our motive for writing is simply this: We want you to enjoy this, too. Your joy will double our joy!
62_1_4.NIV: We write this to make our joy complete.
62_1_4.NRSVCE: We are writing these things so that our joy may be complete.

62_1_5.NKJV: This is the message which we have heard from Him and declare to you, that God is light and in Him is no darkness at all.
62_1_5.KJV: This<S>3778</S> then<S>2532</S> is<S>2076</S> the message<S>1860</S> which<S>3739</S> we have heard<S>191</S> of<S>575</S> him<S>846</S>, and<S>2532</S> declare<S>312</S> unto you<S>5213</S>, that<S>3754</S> God<S>2316</S> is<S>2076</S> light<S>5457</S>, and<S>2532</S> in<S>1722</S> him<S>846</S> is<S>2076</S> no<S>3756</S> darkness<S>4653</S> at all<S>3762</S>.
62_1_5.ESV: This is the message we have heard from him and proclaim to you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.
62_1_5.NASB: This is the message we have heard from Him and announce to you, that God is Light, and in Him there is no darkness at all.
62_1_5.NLT: This is the message we heard from Jesus and now declare to you: God is light, and there is no darkness in him at all.
62_1_5.WEB: This is the message which we have heard from him and announce to you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.
62_1_5.CSB17: This is the message we have heard from him and declare to you: God is light, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and there is absolutely no darkness in him. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
62_1_5.AMP: This is the message [of God's promised revelation] which we have heard from Him and now announce to you, that God is Light [He is holy, His message is truthful, He is perfect in righteousness], and in Him there is no darkness at all [no sin, no wickedness, no imperfection].
62_1_5.MSG: This, in essence, is the message we heard from Christ and are passing on to you: God is light, pure light; there's not a trace of darkness in him.
62_1_5.NIV:
Walking in the Light
This is the message we have heard from him and declare to you: God is light; in him there is no darkness at all.
62_1_5.NRSVCE: This is the message we have heard from him and proclaim to you, that God is light and in him there is no darkness at all.

62_1_6.NKJV: If we say that we have fellowship with Him, and walk in darkness, we lie and do not practice the truth.
62_1_6.KJV: If<S>1437</S> we say<S>2036</S> that<S>3754</S> we have<S>2192</S> fellowship<S>2842</S> with<S>3326</S> him<S>846</S>, and<S>2532</S> walk<S>4043</S> in<S>1722</S> darkness<S>4655</S>, we lie<S>5574</S>, and<S>2532</S> do<S>4160</S> not<S>3756</S> the truth<S>225</S>:
62_1_6.ESV: If we say we have fellowship with him while we walk in darkness, we lie and do not practice the truth.
62_1_6.NASB: If we say that we have fellowship with Him and [yet] walk in the darkness, we lie and do not practice the truth;
62_1_6.NLT: So we are lying if we say we have fellowship with God but go on living in spiritual darkness; we are not practicing the truth.
62_1_6.WEB: If we say that we have fellowship with him and walk in the darkness, we lie, and don’t tell the truth.
62_1_6.CSB17: If we say, “We have fellowship <sup>ⓙ</sup> with him,” and yet we walk in darkness, <sup>ⓚ</sup> we are lying and are not practicing the truth. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
62_1_6.AMP: If we say that we have fellowship with Him and yet walk in the darkness [of sin], we lie and do not practice the truth;
62_1_6.MSG: If we claim that we experience a shared life with him and continue to stumble around in the dark, we're obviously lying through our teeth—we're not living what we claim.
62_1_6.NIV: If we claim to have fellowship with him yet walk in the darkness, we lie and do not live by the truth.
62_1_6.NRSVCE: If we say that we have fellowship with him while we are walking in darkness, we lie and do not do what is true;

62_1_7.NKJV: But if we walk in the light as He is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus Christ His Son cleanses us from all sin.
62_1_7.KJV: But<S>1161</S> if<S>1437</S> we walk<S>4043</S> in<S>1722</S> the light<S>5457</S>, as<S>5613</S> he<S>846</S> is<S>2076</S> in<S>1722</S> the light<S>5457</S>, we have<S>2192</S> fellowship<S>2842</S> one with another<S>3326</S> <S>240</S>, and<S>2532</S> the blood<S>129</S> of Jesus<S>2424</S> Christ<S>5547</S> his<S>846</S> Son<S>5207</S> cleanseth<S>2511</S> us<S>2248</S> from<S>575</S> all<S>3956</S> sin<S>266</S>.
62_1_7.ESV: But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus his Son cleanses us from all sin.
62_1_7.NASB: but if we walk in the Light as He Himself is in the Light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus His Son cleanses us from all sin.
62_1_7.NLT: But if we are living in the light, as God is in the light, then we have fellowship with each other, and the blood of Jesus, his Son, cleanses us from all sin.
62_1_7.WEB: But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus Christ, his Son, cleanses us from all sin.
62_1_7.CSB17: If we walk in the light <sup>ⓜ</sup> as he himself is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus <sup>ⓝ</sup> his Son cleanses us from all sin.
62_1_7.AMP: but if we [really] walk in the Light [that is, live each and every day in conformity with the precepts of God], as He Himself is in the Light, we have [true, unbroken] fellowship with one another [He with us, and we with Him], and the blood of Jesus His Son cleanses us from all sin [by erasing the stain of sin, keeping us cleansed from sin in all its forms and manifestations].
62_1_7.MSG: But if we walk in the light, God himself being the light, we also experience a shared life with one another, as the sacrificed blood of Jesus, God's Son, purges all our sin.
62_1_7.NIV: But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus, his Son, purifies us from all sin.
62_1_7.NRSVCE: but if we walk in the light as he himself is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus his Son cleanses us from all sin.

62_1_8.NKJV: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
62_1_8.KJV: If<S>1437</S> we say<S>2036</S> that<S>3754</S> we have<S>2192</S> no<S>3756</S> sin<S>266</S>, we deceive<S>4105</S> ourselves<S>1438</S>, and<S>2532</S> the truth<S>225</S> is<S>2076</S> not<S>3756</S> in<S>1722</S> us<S>2254</S>.
62_1_8.ESV: If we say we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
62_1_8.NASB: If we say that we have no sin, we are deceiving ourselves and the truth is not in us.
62_1_8.NLT: If we claim we have no sin, we are only fooling ourselves and not living in the truth.
62_1_8.WEB: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
62_1_8.CSB17: If we say, “We have no sin,” <sup>ⓐ</sup> we are deceiving ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
62_1_8.AMP: If we say we have no sin [refusing to admit that we are sinners], we delude ourselves and the truth is not in us. [His word does not live in our hearts.]
62_1_8.MSG: If we claim that we're free of sin, we're only fooling ourselves. A claim like that is errant nonsense.
62_1_8.NIV: If we claim to be without sin, we deceive ourselves and the truth is not in us.
62_1_8.NRSVCE: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.

62_1_9.NKJV: If we confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
62_1_9.KJV: If<S>1437</S> we confess<S>3670</S> our<S>2257</S> sins<S>266</S>, he is<S>2076</S> faithful<S>4103</S> and<S>2532</S> just<S>1342</S> to<S>2443</S> forgive<S>863</S> us<S>2254</S> our sins<S>266</S>, and<S>2532</S> to cleanse<S>2511</S> us<S>2248</S> from<S>575</S> all<S>3956</S> unrighteousness<S>93</S>.
62_1_9.ESV: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
62_1_9.NASB: If we confess our sins, He is faithful and righteous to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
62_1_9.NLT: But if we confess our sins to him, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all wickedness.
62_1_9.WEB: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive us the sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
62_1_9.CSB17: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive <sup>ⓑ</sup> us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
62_1_9.AMP: If we [freely] admit that we have sinned and confess our sins, He is faithful and just [true to His own nature and promises], and will forgive our sins and cleanse us continually from all unrighteousness [our wrongdoing, everything not in conformity with His will and purpose].
62_1_9.MSG: On the other hand, if we admit our sins—make a clean breast of them—he won't let us down; he'll be true to himself. He'll forgive our sins and purge us of all wrongdoing.
62_1_9.NIV: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.
62_1_9.NRSVCE: If we confess our sins, he who is faithful and just will forgive us our sins and cleanse us from all unrighteousness.

62_1_10.NKJV: If we say that we have not sinned, we make Him a liar, and His word is not in us.
62_1_10.ESV: If we say we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.
62_1_10.NASB: If we say that we have not sinned, we make Him a liar and His word is not in us.
62_1_10.NLT: If we claim we have not sinned, we are calling God a liar and showing that his word has no place in our hearts.
62_1_10.WEB: If we say that we haven’t sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.
62_1_10.CSB17: If we say, “We have not sinned,” we make him a liar, and his word <sup>ⓒ</sup> is not in us. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
62_1_10.AMP: If we say that we have not sinned [refusing to admit acts of sin], we make Him [out to be] a liar [by contradicting Him] and His word is not in us.
62_1_10.MSG: If we claim that we've never sinned, we out-and-out contradict God—make a liar out of him. A claim like that only shows off our ignorance of God.
62_1_10.NIV: If we claim we have not sinned, we make him out to be a liar and his word has no place in our lives.
62_1_10.NRSVCE: If we say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.
